title: Sonnet. Death.
content:
It is not death, that sometime in a sigh
    This eloquent breath shall take its speechless flight;
    That sometime these bright stars, that now reply
    In sunlight to the sun, shall set in night;
    That warm conscious flesh shall perish quite,
    And all life's ruddy springs forget to flow;
    That thoughts shall cease, and the immortal sprite
    Be lapp'd in alien clay and laid below;
    It is not death to know this, - but to know
    That pious thoughts, which visit at new graves
    In tender pilgrimage, will cease to go
    So duly and so oft, - and when grass waves
    Over the past-away, there may be then
    No resurrection in the minds of men.
meaning:
Thomas Hood's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Sonnet. Death."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
